title: arrhenatherum elatius subsp. nodosum
intro: Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. nodosum, also known as tall oat-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall stems and delicate white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. nodosum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems.
